theme: blacksmith juggling several workpieces at one forge
title: One Forge, Many Blades: Interleaved Temper Schedule
strategies: always-reheat baseline: reheat immediately before every single operation and process workpieces strictly one at a time, never trusting natural cooling (reproduces the checker's own reference construction)
finish-one-at-a-time recipe: work each piece's full three-operation chain to completion (waiting out its own natural cooling between operations) before touching the next piece in queue

earliest-deadline-first interval choreography: treat each pending operation's temperature window as a closed-form time interval from its own cooling curve, and always run whichever ready piece's interval closes soonest, jumping the idle clock forward to the next interval's opening instead of reheating
reheat only when forced: fall back to reheating a piece only once every remaining piece has already cooled past its window, picking the most-overdue piece first
